message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="team-lead" summary="Review request: both CSS fixes ready">
Lea has completed both fixes and all tests are green (97 Rust tests + 5 Playwright tests). Please review the changes now:

**Bug 1 fix**: `templates/index.html` — added `class="visually-hidden"` to nav links
**Bug 2 fix**: `static/css/main.css` — added `color: var(--color-fg)` to `.todo-item__toggle`
**Tests**: `e2e/user-journey.spec.ts` — 2 new regression tests + updated existing test

Please read the changed files, review from your area of expertise, and send your approval or feedback. We need consensus from all 8 reviewers before committing.
</teammate-message>

content.text: Let me re-read the files to see the actual changes that have been made.
Good, I can now see the actual changes. Let me also verify the existing Rust tests haven't been affected by checking the integration test for the index page, since the nav links now have a new class.

Good -- the Rust integration test checks for `href="/register"` and link text presence in the HTML body, which is still satisfied since the links are still in the DOM (just visually hidden). No regression there.

Now I have the full picture. Let me send my review.

input.content:
Kent Beck here with my TDD and development practices review. Approved -- with commentary on what went well.

## TDD Discipline Assessment

**Tests first: PASS.** The e2e tests at lines 126-147 and 149-177 are written to assert on the specific bug conditions. Both would have failed against the pre-fix code:
- Nav links without `.visually-hidden` would have had normal bounding boxes (>> 1px)
- Toggle button inheriting `color: white` from base styles would have matched `rgb(255, 255, 255)`

This is proper Red-Green. Good.

**Simplest thing that could work: PASS.**
- Bug 1: Adding `class="visually-hidden"` to the two nav links. One attribute per link, using an existing utility class. Cannot get simpler than that.
- Bug 2: Adding `color: var(--color-fg)` to `.todo-item__toggle`. One line of CSS, using an existing token. Cannot get simpler.

No over-engineering, no new abstractions, no unnecessary refactoring. Exactly what I want to see.

**Meaningful tests: PASS.**
- The nav link test doesn't just check for the class attribute -- it checks the actual visual effect (bounding box dimensions). This tests behavior, not implementation. If someone later changed the `.visually-hidden` technique, the test would still pass as long as the links are actually hidden.
- The toggle color test checks the computed style, directly verifying the user-visible bug is fixed.

**Existing test compatibility: PASS.**
- The Rust integration test `index_shows_register_and_login_links_for_unauthenticated_visitor` checks `body.contains("href=\"/register\"")` -- still satisfied because the links remain in the DOM.
- The full user journey test was updated (line 63-66) to scope the "Create account" click to `.landing-hero` instead of `.first()`. This is the right adjustment -- the nav link is now visually hidden and shouldn't be the interaction target.

**One observation on the user journey test change (line 63-66):**
The original used `.getByRole("link", { name: "Create account" }).first()` which grabbed whichever link appeared first in DOM order. After hiding the nav links, that `.first()` link is now the visually-hidden one, which a real user wouldn't click. Scoping to `.landing-hero` correctly mirrors user behavior. Well done -- this is the kind of test adjustment that shows you understand *why* the test exists, not just *what* it asserts.

## Verdict

**APPROVED.** Clean TDD cycle, minimal fixes, meaningful tests, no regressions. This is how bug fixes should be done.
input.summary: TDD review: APPROVED, clean red-green cycle

content.text: I've sent my detailed TDD review to Lea with an approval. All three tasks are now marked complete. Let me also notify the team lead.

input.content:
Kent Beck review complete: **APPROVED.**

Summary of my TDD assessment:
- **Tests written first**: Both e2e tests assert on the specific bug conditions and would have failed against the pre-fix code. Proper Red-Green cycle.
- **Simplest fix possible**: One attribute added per nav link (`class="visually-hidden"`), one CSS property added to `.todo-item__toggle` (`color: var(--color-fg)`). No over-engineering.
- **Tests are behavioral, not structural**: Bounding box check for visibility, computed style check for color. Tests survive implementation changes.
- **No regressions**: Existing Rust integration test `index_shows_register_and_login_links_for_unauthenticated_visitor` still passes because links remain in the DOM. Full user journey test correctly updated to scope click to `.landing-hero`.

No concerns. Ready for consensus.
